title: Agent Pulse
description: One daily evidence-backed decision brief from the coding-agent projects monitored by The Git Reporter.

scope.coverageStatement: V0.2 reports one dated decision brief from direct public source evidence and keeps git motion as a secondary measurement layer. Community feedback metrics are absent until a reproducible feedback collector ships.

inputs.dailyInsight: reported, date-owned Agent Pulse decision brief

editorialRead.headline: Morning Read — July 24, 2026
editorialRead.readHeadline: QwenLM/qwen-code is the first source to inspect.
editorialRead.oneLineSummary: QwenLM/qwen-code leads a 3-item attention queue backed by direct receipts; 24 watchlist sources are fresh, 0 need source-health attention, and movement still requires editorial judgment.
editorialRead.dek: Agent Pulse filtered 24 included projects down to 3 items worth finite newsroom attention. The queue protects attention; it does not declare winners.
bullets: QwenLM/qwen-code — 9 commits today and 1 same-day mapped article, 27.8 below its current daily pace, with 7 hardening-subject points. Inspect the receipt before assigning a story.
NousResearch/hermes-agent — 85 commits landed today. Keep it on watch, but require the next receipt to prove a reader consequence before promoting it.
Kilo-Org/kilocode — No same-day commit after 159 commits in the seven-day window. The pause is notable enough to preserve, not enough to turn into a story.
editorialRead.watchNext: The next meaningful upgrade is feedback flow: issues opened and closed, review latency, stale backlog, and the share of work that starts outside the maintainer team.
editorialRead.caveat: Source-code motion and article coverage are collected. GitHub issue flow, PR review timing, community discussion, and private backlog signals are not.

dailyInsight.headline: Before a daemon passes identity to tools, prove who may receive it
dailyInsight.insight: Qwen Code added a narrow invocation-context path from a daemon-admitted root prompt to Qwen-launched local stdio MCP tools. The source carries a session-and-prompt label for correlation while separately scrubbing the private capability that authenticated the daemon child from tool environments.
dailyInsight.mechanism: The project reconstructs reserved metadata from daemon state, binds it to one root turn, and sends it only over eligible stdio transports. Its design excludes background and subagent paths and non-stdio transports; an end-to-end test observes the metadata at a local echo tool while checking that the private capability is absent.
dailyInsight.whyItMatters: A shared agent runtime can make a tool call traceable without making every downstream tool a holder of the authority that admitted the work. Separating correlation from credentials gives builders a concrete place to test transport scope, turn lifetime, and secret non-propagation.

actions.label: Map the handoff boundary
actions.instruction: For each daemon-to-tool path, write down the identity fields a tool needs and the capability it must never receive. Rebuild reserved metadata at the trusted admission boundary instead of forwarding caller-supplied fields.

actions.label: Test both halves of the contract
actions.instruction: Run one root prompt through an eligible local tool and assert the expected correlation record. In the same test, inspect the tool environment and non-eligible transports to prove the private token and metadata do not leak there.

actions.label: Treat this as source evidence, not deployment evidence
actions.instruction: Before relying on provenance metadata in a shared-session workflow, verify the release you use contains the change and run a non-sensitive local MCP test that captures what the tool receives.

receipts.title: feat(core): propagate trusted daemon invocation context (#7279)
receipts.supports: Shows the source-level invocation-context path, transport plumbing, private-capability sanitization, design note, and end-to-end regression coverage that verifies metadata arrives at a local tool while the private capability does not.

confidence.basis: The public commit, scoped design contract, implementation, and end-to-end local MCP test agree on the intended handoff and secret boundary; the claim stays confined to Qwen Code’s source change.

dailyInsight.limitation: This is one project’s source-level contract. It does not establish that a release or deployment includes the feature, that every MCP server handles metadata safely, or that the design proves a general security property across other agent runtimes.
dailyInsight.falsifier: Revise this call if later source shows the reserved metadata is caller-controlled, if a non-eligible transport receives it, if the private capability reaches a tool environment, or if the change is removed before release.
dailyInsight.watchNext: Watch for release inclusion and a public operator test that exercises the boundary with a real shared session, an eligible local tool, and a non-eligible transport.
